title: erica regia subsp. regia
intro: Erica regia subsp. regia is a plant species in the heath family (Ericaceae) commonly known as the royal or bell heather. It is a flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and wounds, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. The herbal preparations of Erica regia subsp. regia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
